summary.intent: Fix a failing CI pipeline for the moltis project that was encountering build errors related to Vulkan/GLSL shader compilation tools.
summary.outcome: Updated both ci.yml and release.yml to install the full LunarG `vulkan-sdk` package instead of piecemeal Ubuntu packages, resolving both the missing `glslc` package and the Vulkan SDK version mismatch issues.

repo: CI uses LunarG's apt repository for Vulkan tooling — must be set up before the main apt-get install block
llama-cpp-sys-2-0.1.133 requires Vulkan SDK >=1.3.275 (needs LayerSettingsCreateInfoEXT and eMesaDozen) — Ubuntu base repos only ship 1.3.204
Installing vulkan-sdk from LunarG is the correct approach as it bundles libvulkan-dev, headers, and glslang-tools together, avoiding version mismatches

code.finding: Had duplicate identical apt install blocks that both needed updating; LunarG repo setup needed to be moved before the main install step
Mirror of ci.yml Vulkan install pattern — required the same fix

workflow: CI failures should be diagnosed iteratively — first fix revealed a second deeper issue (package existed but wrong version)
When a third-party Rust crate (llama-cpp-sys) pins to a newer SDK API, the CI environment must match — Ubuntu LTS base repos lag significantly behind LunarG releases

friction: Two separate CI runs were required to diagnose the full problem — first run revealed missing glslc, second revealed Vulkan SDK version mismatch
Ubuntu base repos shipping Vulkan 1.3.204 while llama-cpp-sys requires 1.3.275+ is a silent compatibility trap with no clear error until compile time
open_items: Pin the LunarG vulkan-sdk to a specific version in CI to prevent future silent breakage when llama-cpp-sys updates its Vulkan API requirements
Verify release.yml CI passes end-to-end after these changes — only ci.yml run was referenced in the transcript
